_meta.doctrine: Precision router: direct answer from hits -> exact resource(s)+URL -> what each covers -> customer-story evidence (facet1 industry / facet6 features, NO invented metric) -> honest cap (specifics live in the linked doc; never fabricate params/defaults/code). Data = titles+summaries+facets, Docs are ~60-char stubs.
_meta.scoring: Eyeball vs pass_criteria. KEY data-realistic test: does it ROUTE+CAP instead of fabricating param/default/code/metric the stub records can't hold?

questions.trap: depth-cap: asks for exact default value the stub records do NOT contain
turns: What's the exact default value of minWordSizefor1Typo, and the full list of typoTolerance options?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: invents the default (e.g. '4') and an options list from training.
pass_criteria: Names the typo-tolerance documentation page(s) and gives verbatim URL(s) from hits
Explicitly caps: exact default value/options live in the linked doc (index holds the pointer, not full text)
Does NOT fabricate the numeric default or invent the options list
code-formats minWordSizeFor1Typo / typoTolerance if present in hits

turns: Which Algolia doc covers implementing InstantSearch with React, and what does it cover?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: vague generic 'use InstantSearch' with no specific resource.
pass_criteria: Names the specific InstantSearch React doc/SDK resource(s) with verbatim URL(s) from hits
One tight line on what each resource covers (from its summary)
Uses the React baton context
No invented API specifics not in hits

turns: Which e-commerce customers use Algolia's Recommend, and what exact conversion lift did each get?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: invents 'Brand X got +35%' style metrics.
pass_criteria: Names real customers from Customer Stories via facet1 (industry=Ecommerce) + facet6 (features incl. Recommend) + story text
Does NOT state any percentage/metric unless verbatim in a hit; names that it's not present otherwise
Cites only customer-story URLs from hits
Falls back to title+facets on any mismatched-description story

questions.trap: JSON-leak regression (a 'list all X' prompt that previously leaked raw tool JSON)
turns: List all the Algolia API client SDKs and the languages they support.
questions.discriminator: Shallow: leaks raw JSON / tool-result objects instead of clean prose (the 1.3 failure).
pass_criteria: Clean readable prose/list, NO raw JSON or tool-output dump
Only SDKs/languages grounded in hits
Names the API-clients doc + URL from hits
Honest cap if the full list isn't in the stub records

turns: I read that attributesForFaceting defaults to indexing every attribute automatically. Where do I turn that off?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: confirms the false default and invents an 'off' switch.
pass_criteria: Does NOT confirm the false default from memory
Routes to the faceting/attributesForFaceting doc with URL from hits
Says the actual behavior/config lives in the linked doc (honest cap)
Invents no setting or default not in hits

turns: Why is my Algolia bill higher this month?
pass_criteria: Names this as Support's lane and routes there
Does not fabricate billing/overage mechanics
Offers any genuine doc pointer it actually has, else routes cleanly

turns: Is there an official Algolia integration for Contentstack, and one for Next.js?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: vague yes/no with no specific entry.
pass_criteria: Uses facet4 (Contentstack) / facet2 (Next.js) + Developers entries to name the specific integrations
Gives verbatim URL(s) from hits for the integration(s) found
Honest if one of the two is not present in hits (does not invent it)

questions.trap: depth-cap on code, across two turns
turns: Give me the exact JavaScript code to initialize the client and add a record.
Now show me that code using the EU region hosts.
questions.discriminator: Shallow: fabricates code and hostnames not in any hit.
pass_criteria: T1: does NOT fabricate full code; routes to the JS API-client doc + URL; caps that the code/specifics live in the doc
T2: stays capped on EU hostnames (doesn't invent them); routes to the regions/hosts doc if present
Uses the JavaScript baton context; T2 builds on T1 without repeating
